title: ProtocolLab TLS Performance Contracts
purpose: Define implementation-neutral TLS handshake, authentication, early-data, key-update, and bounded record-transfer performance semantics with explicit validity gates.
scope: TLS 1.3 remains the primary performance lane. TLS 1.2 is bounded to an explicit compatibility cell; ChaCha20, mutual authentication, 0-RTT outcomes, KeyUpdate, and record direction/size coverage are separate profile or diagnostic cells.

requirements.title: Exact TLS version
requirements.statement: TLS scenarios MUST request and observe their declared TLS version without silent downgrade; TLS 1.2 is permitted only in the explicit compatibility contract.

requirements.title: Full handshake semantics
requirements.statement: A full-handshake scenario MUST establish a fresh authenticated TLS session without offered or accepted resumption state.

requirements.title: Resumed handshake semantics
requirements.statement: A resumed-handshake scenario MUST prove accepted TLS 1.3 PSK resumption and preserve the source-session provenance without sending 0-RTT application data.

requirements.title: TLS cryptographic and identity profile
requirements.statement: TLS scenarios MUST declare SNI, ALPN, certificate-chain profile, cipher suite, key-exchange group, signature algorithm, session-reuse mode, and platform or hardware-acceleration provenance as comparison metadata.

requirements.title: TLS validity gate
requirements.statement: TLS measurements MUST require successful handshake or record completion, expected negotiated parameters, deterministic application-data bytes when present, and zero unexpected failures or timeouts.

requirements.title: TLS metrics and artifacts
requirements.statement: TLS scenarios MUST declare handshake rate or byte throughput, latency percentiles, transferred bytes, completion, failure and timeout counts, and raw negotiation, validation, stdout, stderr, and normalized-result artifacts as applicable.

requirements.title: TLS comparison isolation
requirements.statement: TLS comparisons MUST match exact TLS version, handshake mode, cryptographic profile, certificate-chain profile, ALPN, role, execution profile, platform, topology, load, network, and repetition policy.

requirements.title: TLS 1.2 compatibility boundary
requirements.statement: TLS 1.2 MUST use its dedicated compatibility profile, exact ECDHE-ECDSA AES-128-GCM suite, no 0-RTT or KeyUpdate semantics, and MUST NOT be aggregated with TLS 1.3 comparison cells.

requirements.title: TLS 1.3 ChaCha20 profile
requirements.statement: ChaCha20 measurements MUST use the exact TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256 profile and remain a distinct comparison cell from AES-GCM.

requirements.title: Mutual TLS authentication
requirements.statement: Mutual TLS MUST require and validate both server and client certificate profiles and record both leaf DER hashes without publishing private keys.

requirements.title: Bounded 0-RTT outcomes
requirements.statement: Accepted and rejected 0-RTT MUST be separate cells using identical replay-safe deterministic input, proven single-use ticket provenance, explicit acceptance or rejection, and exactly-once post-handshake recovery when rejected.

requirements.title: KeyUpdate diagnostic
requirements.statement: The KeyUpdate diagnostic MUST use TLS 1.3 on a proven connection, request exactly one client-initiated update, prove the traffic-secret transition without publishing secrets, and complete deterministic post-update data.

requirements.title: Record direction and size coverage
requirements.statement: Record coverage MUST execute the canonical client-to-server and server-to-client 1 KiB, 64 KiB, and 1 MiB plaintext cases with per-case byte counts and payload hashes, without prescribing TLS wire-record segmentation.
